response.justification: Despite a strong full-stack and open-source background, the candidate has minimal C++ experience (only one brief role at National Instruments) and no demonstrable expertise in core requirements like message brokers, IEC 62443, secure SDLC, or industrial IoT protocols — all critical for this role. Their recent work is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of high-performance C++ system design or multi-threaded industrial applications.
strengths: Extensive full-stack experience with diverse technologies and open-source contributions
Strong track record in agile practices, testing, and developer mentorship
Proven ability to work on distributed, event-driven systems and infrastructure
concerns: Lacks recent or substantial C++ development experience required for core responsibilities
No evidence of exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or SDL practices
No demonstrated experience with message brokers, MQTT, or industrial protocols like OPC UA or Modbus
